_meta.description: 10 orthogonal style dimensions on 1-5 scales. Each dimension captures a continuous spectrum of writing style. The synthesizer maps interview answers to positions on these scales.
_meta.usage: Dimensions modify registers. A register sets the baseline; dimensions fine-tune it. Half-point values (e.g., 3.5) are valid for nuance.

dimensions.name: Formality

left_pole.label: Frozen/Formal
left_pole.description: No contractions, full titles, institutional voice, careful distance

right_pole.label: Casual/Intimate
right_pole.description: Contractions, first names, slang-adjacent, personal disclosure

dimensions.description: How formally structured the language is, from institutional register to intimate conversation.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: If your writing were a dress code, is it black-tie or jeans-and-sneakers?

dimensions.name: Density

left_pole.label: Sparse/Accessible
left_pole.description: One idea per paragraph, plain language, lots of white space

right_pole.label: Dense/Technical
right_pole.description: Multi-clause sentences, specialized vocabulary, high information-per-word

dimensions.description: Information density per unit of text. Low density prioritizes comprehension; high density prioritizes thoroughness.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: Do your readers need breathing room between ideas, or can they handle fire-hose delivery?

dimensions.name: Warmth

left_pole.label: Analytical/Detached
left_pole.description: Objective stance, minimal emotional language, lets data speak

right_pole.label: Warm/Empathetic
right_pole.description: Acknowledges feelings, uses we/you, shows care for reader experience

dimensions.description: The emotional temperature of the writing, from clinical objectivity to genuine human warmth.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: When you share findings, do you present data or tell people why it matters to them?

dimensions.name: Pace

left_pole.label: Expansive/Deliberate
left_pole.description: Takes time to develop ideas, rich context, patient unfolding

right_pole.label: Concise/Compressed
right_pole.description: Every word earns its place, high signal-to-noise, no padding

dimensions.description: How quickly the writing moves through ideas. Expansive writing develops context; compressed writing trusts the reader.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: When you edit your own writing, do you usually add context or cut words?

dimensions.name: Structure

left_pole.label: Flowing/Narrative
left_pole.description: Connected prose, transitions between ideas, essay-like

right_pole.label: Modular/Scannable
right_pole.description: Headers, bullets, numbered lists, standalone sections

dimensions.description: How the text is organized, from continuous flowing prose to discrete modular chunks.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: Do you prefer text you read linearly or text you can jump around in?

dimensions.name: Voice Person

left_pole.label: Impersonal/Third-Person
left_pole.description: The author is invisible, passive constructions, no I/we

right_pole.label: First-Person/Direct
right_pole.description: Strong authorial I, opinions stated directly, personal perspective

dimensions.description: How visible the author is in the text, from invisible institution to unmistakable individual.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: Do readers know who you are from your writing, or could anyone have written it?

dimensions.name: Authority

right_pole.label: Authoritative/Declarative
right_pole.description: Confident assertions, clear positions, expert stance

dimensions.description: How confidently the writing asserts its claims, from tentative exploration to expert declaration.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: Do you present your ideas as discoveries-in-progress or as conclusions you stand behind?

dimensions.name: Abstraction

left_pole.label: Concrete/Grounded
left_pole.description: Examples, case studies, tangible references, specific details

right_pole.label: Abstract/Conceptual
right_pole.description: Theoretical frameworks, generalized principles, systemic thinking

dimensions.description: Whether the writing lives in the world of specifics or the world of ideas.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: Do you explain ideas through real examples or through underlying principles?

dimensions.name: Playfulness

left_pole.label: Serious/Restrained
left_pole.description: No humor, no wordplay, gravity maintained throughout

right_pole.label: Playful/Irreverent
right_pole.description: Wordplay, wit, unexpected juxtapositions, tonal surprises

dimensions.description: How much room the writing makes for humor, wit, and tonal play.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: When you make a serious point, do you ever sneak in a joke or unexpected aside?

dimensions.name: Context Dependence

left_pole.label: Explicit/Self-Contained
left_pole.description: Defines all terms, no assumed knowledge, stands alone

right_pole.label: Contextual/Allusive
right_pole.description: References shared knowledge, allusions, assumes cultural literacy

dimensions.description: How much the writing relies on shared context versus being self-contained.
dimensions.diagnostic_question: Do you write for someone who just walked in, or for someone already in the conversation?
